title: New York State Route 256

(**NY 256**) is a north–south [state highway](/wiki/State_highway "State highway") located within [Livingston County, New York](/wiki/Livingston_County,_New_York "Livingston County, New York"), in the United States. It extends for 21.08 miles (33.92 km) across mostly rural terrain from an intersection with [NY 63](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_63 "New York State Route 63") in the village of [Dansville](/wiki/Dansville,_Livingston_County,_New_York "Dansville, Livingston County, New York") to a junction with [NY 15](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_15 "New York State Route 15") on the [Geneseo](/wiki/Geneseo_(town),_New_York "Geneseo (town), New York")–[Livonia](/wiki/Livonia_(town),_New_York "Livonia (town), New York") town line. The northern half of NY 256, named West Lake Road, passes along the western shore of [Conesus Lake](/wiki/Conesus_Lake "Conesus Lake"). NY 256 was assigned as part of the [1930 renumbering of state highways in New York](/wiki/1930_state_highway_renumbering_(New_York) "1930 state highway renumbering (New York)") to an alignment extending from [Groveland](/wiki/Groveland,_New_York "Groveland, New York") to modern [U.S. Route 20A](/wiki/U.S._Route_20A_(New_York) "U.S. Route 20A (New York)") (US 20A) west of [Lakeville](/wiki/Lakeville,_New_York "Lakeville, New York"). It was extended north to its current northern terminus c. 1940 and south to Dansville in stages during the 1930s and 1940s.

State highway in Livingston County, New York, US

**New York State Route 256** (**NY 256**) is a north–south [state highway](/wiki/State_highway "State highway") located within [Livingston County, New York](/wiki/Livingston_County,_New_York "Livingston County, New York"), in the United States. It extends for 21.08 miles (33.92 km) across mostly rural terrain from an intersection with [NY 63](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_63 "New York State Route 63") in the village of [Dansville](/wiki/Dansville,_Livingston_County,_New_York "Dansville, Livingston County, New York") to a junction with [NY 15](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_15 "New York State Route 15") on the [Geneseo](/wiki/Geneseo_(town),_New_York "Geneseo (town), New York")–[Livonia](/wiki/Livonia_(town),_New_York "Livonia (town), New York") town line. The northern half of NY 256, named West Lake Road, passes along the western shore of [Conesus Lake](/wiki/Conesus_Lake "Conesus Lake"). NY 256 was assigned as part of the [1930 renumbering of state highways in New York](/wiki/1930_state_highway_renumbering_(New_York) "1930 state highway renumbering (New York)") to an alignment extending from [Groveland](/wiki/Groveland,_New_York "Groveland, New York") to modern [U.S. Route 20A](/wiki/U.S._Route_20A_(New_York) "U.S. Route 20A (New York)") (US 20A) west of [Lakeville](/wiki/Lakeville,_New_York "Lakeville, New York"). It was extended north to its current northern terminus c. 1940 and south to Dansville in stages during the 1930s and 1940s.

## Route description

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=New_York_State_Route_256&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Route description")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/1/1a/NY_256_in_Dansville.jpg/250px-NY_256_in_Dansville.jpg)](/wiki/File:NY_256_in_Dansville.jpg)

NY 256 through Dansville

NY 256 begins at an intersection with [NY 63](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_63 "New York State Route 63") in the village of [Dansville](/wiki/Dansville,_Livingston_County,_New_York "Dansville, Livingston County, New York"). It initially heads northeastward on Perine Street, following it past five residential blocks to a junction with Health Street on the northeastern edge of the village. Perine Street ends here; however, NY 256 continues to the north on Health Street, following the street out of both Dansville and the [Canaseraga Creek](/wiki/Canaseraga_Creek "Canaseraga Creek") valley that contains the village. Outside of the valley, the route travels north through the rural towns of [North Dansville](/wiki/North_Dansville,_New_York "North Dansville, New York") and [Sparta](/wiki/Sparta,_New_York "Sparta, New York"), passing by farmland on its way to a junction with [County Route 71](/wiki/County_Route_71_(Livingston_County,_New_York) "County Route 71 (Livingston County, New York)") (CR 71),[[3]](#cite_note-bing-3) once designated as [NY 255](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_255 "New York State Route 255").[[4]](#cite_note-4) While CR 71 heads northeast toward the [hamlet](/wiki/Hamlet_(New_York) "Hamlet (New York)") of Conesus, NY 256 continues northwest to serve Scottsburg, a small community in the far northwestern corner of the town of Sparta.[[3]](#cite_note-bing-3)

North of Scottsburg, NY 256 heads into the town of [Conesus](/wiki/Conesus,_New_York "Conesus, New York") and enters the south end of the [Conesus Lake](/wiki/Conesus_Lake "Conesus Lake") valley. Here, it follows the west bank of Conesus Inlet past a marshy area that eventually leads to the southern end of Conesus Lake. The route continues on, following the western lakeshore through the towns of [Groveland](/wiki/Groveland,_New_York "Groveland, New York") and [Geneseo](/wiki/Geneseo_(town),_New_York "Geneseo (town), New York") as West Lake Road. Along this stretch, NY 256 serves many lakeside homes and communities. At Graywood, a community 1.75 miles (2.82 km) south of the lake's north end, NY 256 joins the Geneseo–[Livonia](/wiki/Livonia_(town),_New_York "Livonia (town), New York") town line and follows it for the remainder of its routing. From this point north, the route traverses lightly populated areas dominated by farmland. NY 256 crosses [US 20A](/wiki/U.S._Route_20A_(New_York) "U.S. Route 20A (New York)") west of [Lakeville](/wiki/Lakeville,_New_York "Lakeville, New York")—a hamlet situated at the northern tip of Conesus Lake—before ending about 1.5 miles (2.4 km) later at a junction with [NY 15](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_15 "New York State Route 15").[[3]](#cite_note-bing-3)

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=New_York_State_Route_256&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: History")]

NY 256 was assigned as part of the [1930 renumbering of state highways in New York](/wiki/1930_state_highway_renumbering_(New_York) "1930 state highway renumbering (New York)"). It initially began at [NY 36A](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_36A "New York State Route 36A") (now [NY 63](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_63 "New York State Route 63")) in the [hamlet](/wiki/Hamlet_(New_York) "Hamlet (New York)") of [Groveland](/wiki/Groveland,_New_York "Groveland, New York") and followed Scottsburg Road east to Scottsburg, where it turned north to follow West Lake Road to a terminus at [NY 254](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_254_(1930%E2%80%931940) "New York State Route 254 (1930–1940)") (now [US 20A](/wiki/U.S._Route_20A_(New_York) "U.S. Route 20A (New York)")) west of [Lakeville](/wiki/Lakeville,_New_York "Lakeville, New York").[[2]](#cite_note-gb-2) NY 256 was rerouted c. 1936 to follow a new highway connecting Scottsburg to [NY 255](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_255 "New York State Route 255") southeast of the hamlet.[[5]](#cite_note-5)[[6]](#cite_note-6) The route was extended north to [US 15](/wiki/U.S._Route_15_in_New_York "U.S. Route 15 in New York") (modern [NY 15](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_15 "New York State Route 15")) c. 1940[[7]](#cite_note-7)[[8]](#cite_note-1940map-8) and south to [Dansville](/wiki/Dansville,_Livingston_County,_New_York "Dansville, Livingston County, New York") by way of NY 255 in December 1940. NY 255 was then truncated to its junction with NY 256.[[9]](#cite_note-ny36acanned-9) On April 1, 1983, ownership and maintenance of NY 256 between [NY 63](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_63 "New York State Route 63") and Depot Road (three blocks north of Perine Street) in Dansville was transferred from [Livingston County](/wiki/Livingston_County,_New_York "Livingston County, New York") to the state of [New York](/wiki/New_York_(state) "New York (state)") as part of a highway maintenance swap between the two levels of government.[[10]](#cite_note-10)

## Major intersections

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=New_York_State_Route_256&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Major intersections")]

The entire route is in [Livingston County](/wiki/Livingston_County,_New_York "Livingston County, New York").
